summary: This notebook implements a multi-stage inference pipeline for the PhysioNet ECG Image Digitization competition. It sequentially applies three neural networks to correct image geometry, rectify the ECG trace, and predict pixel-level coordinates that are converted into time-series waveforms. The author demonstrates how to adapt a previous version to a competition hint and higher resolution by updating the network architecture, preprocessing, and postprocessing steps. Readers should understand how to chain image processing stages, handle coordinate-to-series conversion, and manage submission formatting for multi-lead ECG data.
competition_flow: Loads test ECG images, processes them through three sequential neural networks for geometric correction and coordinate prediction, converts the predicted coordinates into 12-lead time-series waveforms, and exports a formatted CSV submission.
data_reading: Images are loaded via cv2.imread as RGB arrays.
Metadata and test splits are read with pd.read_csv.
Intermediate model outputs are loaded via np.load.
data_processing: Applies homography-based normalization and gridpoint rectification for geometric correction.
Crops the image to a fixed ROI, resizes to (1696, 4352) using bilinear interpolation, and normalizes pixel values by dividing by 255.
Uses linear interpolation to match target row counts during submission assembly.
models: Net3 (ResNet34 encoder with custom MyCoordUnetDecoder and Conv2d head)
insights: Multi-stage geometric correction (homography followed by gridpoint rectification) is required before coordinate prediction to handle ECG image distortions.
Coordinate outputs must be scaled and offset using competition-specific constants (zero_mv, mv_to_pixel) to reconstruct valid ECG series.
Submission formatting requires expanding 4-channel predictions to 12 leads and interpolating sequences to match the exact row counts specified in the test metadata.
